ChangeRequest.name: Joint liabilities according to Anacredit
ChangeRequest.comment:
# Overview
Joint liabilities seems to be only partially illustrated in the LDM, as they are only available for Other Loans while they should be possible for all the type of instruments envisaged for Anacredit reporting.
### Contact person @Silvia.Giacinti
### Business definition In AnaCredit, the `joint liability amount` is an attribute that is required for instruments that involve a plurality of debtors, meaning two or more counterparties have an unconditional obligation to make repayments arising from the same instrument. This applies regardless of whether each debtor is fully or partially liable. The joint liabilities dataset (Dataset 5), which includes the `joint liability amount` is specifically reported for instruments that meet this multi-debtor condition. While the `joint liability amount` is an attribute of the debtor's responsibility rather than an instrument type itself, any instrument classified under Article 1(23) of the AnaCredit Regulation can potentially admit a joint liability amount if it has multiple debtors. These instrument types are: These instrument types are: - Reverse repurchase agreements - Deposits other than reverse repurchase agreements - Overdraft - Credit card debt - Revolving credit other than overdrafts and credit card debt - Credit lines other than revolving credit - Trade receivables - Financial leases - Other loans
### Legal definition Regulation EU/2016/867 Template 1 Point 5 ![image](/uploads/2ad12423b9bbf6fe649fa7ec1533ccb1/image.png)
### Reason for inclusion Anacredit transformation rules
### Relevant Framework/Template Anacredit
### Additional documentation/links (optional) Anacredit Manual - Part II Chapter 7
Anacredit Manual - Part III Chapter 3
### Before closing checklist - [ ] Assignee for review - [ ] Assignee to close - [ ] Other actions
